title: CryoSaver Current Leads
note: Incumbent catalog product: CryoSaver HTS current leads, 'over a thousand leads in the field' for 10+ years; standard and hermetic variants, 150A-2000A.

title: HTS-110 Current Leads
note: US distribution with published pricing (FCA San Carlos, CA) and quantity discounts — leads are already a catalog-and-distributor commodity.

title: High Temperature Superconducting (HTS) Current Leads - Global Market
note: Fetched. Global HTS current leads market US$123M (2024) -> US$164M (2031), 4.2% CAGR. Key players: Hall Scientific, E2P, DABS, Furukawa Electric, Solid Material Solutions, Brookhaven Technology Group, CAN Superconductors.

title: Development of ITER HTS Current Lead at ASIPP
note: ASIPP (CAS Institute of Plasma Physics) has designed/fabricated/tested ITER HTS current leads (68/55/10 kA classes) in-house since 2007 — the biggest lead procurements go to state labs, not merchant vendors.

title: Splice joint resistances of commercial REBCO-coated conductors and their reduction
note: Splice resistance is dominated by commercial tape architecture (interface/stabilizer); reduction techniques are published openly — thin process moat for a merchant joiner.

title: 以超导电缆为应用先锋，上海历时18年打造全球最全高温超导产业链
note: Shanghai's 18-year full-chain HTS industrial buildout (上海超导 et al.) — Chinese domestic component self-supply, including leads/terminations, is policy-backed.

title: MT27: BSCCO geometrical and soldering optimization for high current lead applications
note: Major magnet integrator develops and optimizes its own current leads — integrators make, not buy.

title: HTS (High Temperature Superconductor) Products
note: US magnet-system builder already bundles HTS leads with its systems — leads captured as an integrator attach, not a merchant component.

title: Superconducting joints for MRI magnet in persistent mode
note: Persistent-current joints (PbBi solder on NbTi/Nb3Sn) are the standard, long-guarded in-house method of the MRI magnet industry — closed to third-party component vendors.

title: Novel Superconducting Joints for Persistent Mode Magnet Applications
note: True superconducting REBCO joints 'not realized stably and reproducibly yet' — the high-value version of the product is still a research problem, not a shippable component.
